output:
Aggregated execute batches: completed 12/12.
Batch 1: Batch 1 complete. T1 audit confirmed that `_classify_confirmation` is only called from `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:1022` and the deterministic keyword tests in `tests/test_admin_payments.py:597-607`; `self._classifier_client` has no live readers beyond its initialization in `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:65`; the admin-chat-local dead symbols remain scoped to `src/features/admin_chat/` plus the flagged tests; and the same-name `_can_user_message_bot` in `src/features/admin/admin_cog.py:211,363` is a separate DM-routing feature that remains live and must stay untouched.
Batch 2: Batch 2 complete. `_classify_confirmation` was renamed in place to `_classify_confirmation_keyword` in `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:97`, the current synchronous caller in `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:1022` was re-pointed to preserve existing behavior until the async wrapper lands in later batches, and the direct keyword tests in `tests/test_admin_payments.py:597-607` now call the renamed helper. Verification passed for `tests/test_admin_payments.py` and for the full repository suite.
Batch 3: Batch 3 complete. Added `_CLASSIFIER_TOOL` and `_CLASSIFIER_SYSTEM_PROMPT` at `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:22-58`, and added the async `_classify_confirmation` wrapper at `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:183-228`. The wrapper uses `self._classifier_client.messages.create(...)` with forced tool use, preserves fail-closed keyword fallback when the client is missing, when the API raises, when the response lacks a valid tool-use payload, and when the classifier returns an unknown value. Verification passed for `tests/test_admin_payments.py` and for the full repository suite.
Batch 4: Batch 4 complete. `_handle_pending_recipient_message` now unpacks `(classification, clarification_reply)` from `await self._classify_confirmation(content)` and routes via explicit `if/elif` branches in `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:1113-1125`. The ambiguous handler signature was minimally widened at `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:617-624` to accept the forwarded `clarification_reply` kwarg without changing behavior before T5. Verification passed for `tests/test_admin_payments.py` and for the full repository suite.
Batch 5: Batch 5 complete. `_handle_test_receipt_ambiguous` now sends a recipient-facing clarification message on the first ambiguous reply and preserves the two-strike escalation behavior. The handler uses the LLM-provided `clarification_reply` when present, otherwise it sends the default `confirmed/yes/not received` prompt. Verification passed for `tests/test_admin_payments.py` and for the full repository suite.
Batch 6: Batch 6 complete. `_handle_test_receipt_negative` now sends a recipient-directed message before the existing admin-tag detail message, preserving the existing manual-review detail content and the admin DM path. Verification passed for `tests/test_admin_payments.py` and for the full repository suite.
Batch 7: Batch 7 complete. Removed the dead admin-chat-only `_is_directed_at_bot`, `_can_user_message_bot`, `_message_access_cache`, and the staged-cleanup retention comment from `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py`. Cleared the corresponding stale test references in `tests/test_admin_payments.py` so the scoped grep target is fully clean. Verification passed for `tests/test_admin_payments.py`, the scoped grep, and the full repository suite.
Batch 8: Batch 8 complete. Removed the member-only prompt/conversation/tool-selection dead path from `src/features/admin_chat/agent.py`, collapsed the agent onto the admin prompt/tool flow, and updated the `admin_chat_cog.py` call site to match the simplified `chat()` signature. Verification passed for `tests/test_admin_payments.py`, the scoped agent/cog grep, and the full repository suite.
Batch 9: Batch 9 completed. Removed the dead role-based tool registration helpers from `src/features/admin_chat/tools.py`, deleted `ALL_TOOL_NAMES` because it had no remaining external readers after that cleanup, and updated the two directly affected social-route tests to assert against `admin_tools.TOOLS` instead. Verification passed with `pytest tests/test_social_route_tools.py --tb=no -q --no-header` and the full suite `pytest --tb=no -q --no-header`. Checkpoint written to `.megaplan/plans/follow-up-cleanup-pass-after-20260411-0608/execution_batch_9.json`.
Batch 10: Batch 10 completed without additional source edits. The planned T10 test updates were already present in the working tree: `tests/test_social_route_tools.py` uses `admin_tools.TOOLS`, the approved-member routing test is gone, the non-admin fall-through test no longer mocks `_can_user_message_bot`, the ambiguous escalation test expects the first clarification message, and the negative-path test expects two channel messages plus the admin DM. Verification passed with `pytest tests/test_admin_payments.py --tb=no -q --no-header`, `pytest tests/test_social_route_tools.py --tb=no -q --no-header`, and the full suite `pytest --tb=no -q --no-header`. Checkpoint written to `.megaplan/plans/follow-up-cleanup-pass-after-20260411-0608/execution_batch_10.json`.
Batch 11: Batch 11 completed. Added new coverage in `tests/test_admin_payments.py` for the LLM classifier mappings (`confirmed`, `not_received`, `unclear` with reply), keyword fallback when the API key is missing, keyword fallback when the LLM call raises, first ambiguous-reply clarification with both the default text and an LLM-supplied reply, the recipient-facing negative-path notice plus admin DM, and module-level dead-code removal assertions against `admin_agent`, `admin_tools`, and `AdminChatCog`. Verification passed with `pytest tests/test_admin_payments.py --tb=no -q --no-header` and the full suite `pytest --tb=no -q --no-header`. Checkpoint written to `.megaplan/plans/follow-up-cleanup-pass-after-20260411-0608/execution_batch_11.json`.
Batch 12: Batch 12 completed. The required validation sequence passed in order: `pytest tests/test_admin_payments.py -x -q` (`59 passed, 1 warning`), `pytest tests/test_social_route_tools.py -x -q` (`27 passed, 4 warnings`), and full `pytest -x -q` (`209 passed, 4 warnings`). The final grep sweep is clean: `MEMBER_SYSTEM_PROMPT`, `MEMBER_MAX_CONVERSATION_LENGTH`, `MEMBER_TOOLS`, `ADMIN_ONLY_TOOLS`, `get_tools_for_role`, and `_is_directed_at_bot` return zero hits across `src/` and `tests/`, while `_can_user_message_bot` remains only in `src/features/admin/admin_cog.py` as expected. A throwaway script reproduced the mocked LLM `unclear` path end-to-end for `ok thanks`, verified the bot posted the LLM-supplied clarification reply, and was then deleted. Checkpoint written to `.megaplan/plans/follow-up-cleanup-pass-after-20260411-0608/execution_batch_12.json`.

task_updates.executor_notes: Audit-only batch; no source edits required. Verified `_classify_confirmation` has one production caller at `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:1022` and test-only direct callers at `tests/test_admin_payments.py:600,606,607`. Verified `self._classifier_client` is initialized at `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:65` and has zero current readers anywhere else in the repo. Verified admin-chat-local `_can_user_message_bot`, `_is_directed_at_bot`, `MEMBER_SYSTEM_PROMPT`, `MEMBER_TOOLS`, `ADMIN_ONLY_TOOLS`, `get_tools_for_role`, `MEMBER_MAX_CONVERSATION_LENGTH`, and the `is_admin` plumbing are only referenced inside `src/features/admin_chat/` plus `tests/test_admin_payments.py` and `tests/test_social_route_tools.py`. Separately verified `src/features/admin/admin_cog.py:211,363` contains a different live `_can_user_message_bot` used for DM routing and it remains out of scope.

Renamed the existing classmethod in place from `_classify_confirmation` to `_classify_confirmation_keyword` at `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:97` with the body, decorator, and return type unchanged. Re-pointed the current synchronous production caller at `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:1022` so this intermediate batch remains behaviorally intact before T3/T4 introduce the async wrapper. Re-pointed the deterministic keyword tests at `tests/test_admin_payments.py:600,606,607` to the new helper name without changing assertions. Verification: `pytest tests/test_admin_payments.py --tb=no -q --no-header` passed; `pytest --tb=no -q --no-header` passed with `201 passed, 4 warnings`.

Added module-level classifier constants `_CLASSIFIER_TOOL` and `_CLASSIFIER_SYSTEM_PROMPT` at `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:22-58`. Added async instance method `_classify_confirmation` at `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:183-228` returning `(classification, clarification_reply)`. Verified the method returns `(keyword_result, None)` when `self._classifier_client` is `None`, calls `self._classifier_client.messages.create(...)` with `model=self._classifier_model`, `max_tokens=256`, `system=_CLASSIFIER_SYSTEM_PROMPT`, `tools=[_CLASSIFIER_TOOL]`, `tool_choice={"type": "tool", "name": "classify_payment_reply"}`, and `messages=[{"role": "user", "content": content or ""}]`, logs and falls back to keywords on exception with `exc_info=True`, falls back on missing or malformed tool-use input, falls back on unknown classification strings, and maps `confirmed -> positive`, `not_received -> negative`, `unclear -> ambiguous` while propagating a non-empty `reply` string. Verification: `pytest tests/test_admin_payments.py --tb=no -q --no-header` passed; `pytest --tb=no -q --no-header` passed with `201 passed, 4 warnings`.

Replaced the dict/getattr-based dispatch in `_handle_pending_recipient_message` with explicit branching at `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:1113-1125`. The method now does `classification, clarification_reply = await self._classify_confirmation(content)`, routes `positive` to `_handle_test_receipt_positive`, routes `negative` to `_handle_test_receipt_negative`, and routes the remaining path to `_handle_test_receipt_ambiguous(..., clarification_reply=clarification_reply)`. Added the minimal kw-only `clarification_reply: Optional[str] = None` parameter to `_handle_test_receipt_ambiguous` at `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:617-624` so the forwarded kwarg is accepted before T5 starts using it; handler behavior is otherwise unchanged in this batch. Verification: `pytest tests/test_admin_payments.py --tb=no -q --no-header` passed; `pytest --tb=no -q --no-header` passed with `201 passed, 4 warnings`.

Updated `_handle_test_receipt_ambiguous` at `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:617-646` so that after incrementing the ambiguous reply counter, it sends a recipient-facing in-thread clarification when the post-increment count is `< 2`. When `clarification_reply` is provided, the handler sends `f"<@{recipient_user_id}> {clarification_reply}"`; otherwise it sends the default clarification asking for `confirmed`/`yes` or `not received`. The existing `>= 2` escalation still delegates directly to `_handle_test_receipt_negative(updated_intent)` with no `clarification_reply` forwarding. Updated the existing ambiguous-path regression test at `tests/test_admin_payments.py:1527-1530` to assert the first clarification message is sent. Verification: `pytest tests/test_admin_payments.py --tb=no -q --no-header` passed; `pytest --tb=no -q --no-header` passed with `201 passed, 4 warnings`.

Updated `_handle_test_receipt_negative` at `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:593-603` to send a recipient-facing notice before the existing admin-tag detail send. The new first send tells the recipient they have been flagged for admin review and to wait for `self._admin_mention`; the existing detail send remains unchanged and still carries the intent/test-payment/signature/wallet detail; the `_notify_intent_admin(...)` DM path remains unchanged and still runs after the status update. Updated the direct negative-path regression test at `tests/test_admin_payments.py:1450-1456` to assert two channel messages in order: recipient notice first, existing detail second. Verification: `pytest tests/test_admin_payments.py --tb=no -q --no-header` passed; `pytest --tb=no -q --no-header` passed with `201 passed, 4 warnings`.

Removed the `_message_access_cache` field init from `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:93-99` while preserving `_guild_context_cache` and `_ACCESS_CACHE_TTL_SECONDS`. Deleted the dead `_is_directed_at_bot` and `_can_user_message_bot` methods from the same file, leaving `_is_admin` and `_resolve_context_guild_id` intact at `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:977-1008`. Removed the staged-cleanup retention comment from `_handle_admin_message`. To satisfy the batch's scoped-grep requirement, also removed the stale `_can_user_message_bot` test references and the now-redundant `test_approved_member_no_longer_routed_to_agent` from `tests/test_admin_payments.py`. Verification: `rg -n "_can_user_message_bot|_is_directed_at_bot|_message_access_cache" src/features/admin_chat tests` returned zero hits; `pytest tests/test_admin_payments.py --tb=no -q --no-header` passed with `50 passed, 1 warning`; `pytest --tb=no -q --no-header` passed with `200 passed, 4 warnings`. `src/features/admin/admin_cog.py` was not modified.

Removed `MEMBER_SYSTEM_PROMPT` and `MEMBER_MAX_CONVERSATION_LENGTH` from `src/features/admin_chat/agent.py`, switched the import at `src/features/admin_chat/agent.py:15` from `get_tools_for_role` to `TOOLS`, simplified `_trim_conversation` at `src/features/admin_chat/agent.py:202-227` to always use `ADMIN_MAX_CONVERSATION_LENGTH`, and dropped the `is_admin` parameter from `AdminChatAgent.chat()` at `src/features/admin_chat/agent.py:229-236`. Inside `chat()`, `available_tools` is now always `TOOLS`, the system prompt is always `SYSTEM_PROMPT` with the existing server-config override preserved, `_POM_ADDENDUM` is always appended, `_ADMIN_IDENTITY_INJECTED_TOOLS` injection is preserved without the old `is_admin` guard, and `execute_tool(...)` now always receives `requester_id=None` as required. Updated the single cog call site at `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:1200-1206` to drop `is_admin=True` while keeping `requester_id=None`. Verification: `rg -n "MEMBER_SYSTEM_PROMPT|MEMBER_MAX_CONVERSATION_LENGTH|get_tools_for_role|is_admin|requester_id=None if is_admin|_trim_conversation\(" src/features/admin_chat/agent.py src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py` showed only the expected non-agent `_is_admin` references in `admin_chat_cog.py`; `pytest tests/test_admin_payments.py --tb=no -q --no-header` passed with `50 passed, 1 warning`; `pytest --tb=no -q --no-header` passed with `200 passed, 4 warnings`.

Removed `MEMBER_TOOLS`, `ADMIN_ONLY_TOOLS`, the two role-classification asserts, and `get_tools_for_role` from `src/features/admin_chat/tools.py`. A fresh grep across `src` and `tests` showed `ALL_TOOL_NAMES` had no external readers once those helpers were removed, so it was deleted rather than rewritten. Updated the two affected assertions in `tests/test_social_route_tools.py` to derive registered tool names from `admin_tools.TOOLS` directly. Verification: `rg -n "MEMBER_TOOLS|ADMIN_ONLY_TOOLS|get_tools_for_role|ALL_TOOL_NAMES" src tests` returned zero hits; `pytest tests/test_social_route_tools.py --tb=no -q --no-header` passed with `27 passed, 4 warnings`; `pytest --tb=no -q --no-header` passed with `200 passed, 4 warnings`. `_ADMIN_IDENTITY_INJECTED_TOOLS`, requester_id handling in `execute_tool`, and the query/batch/upsert implementations were left untouched.

No additional source edits were required in this batch because the listed test updates were already present in the working tree from prior batches. Verified that `tests/test_social_route_tools.py` now asserts against `admin_tools.TOOLS`, `test_approved_member_no_longer_routed_to_agent` is absent, `test_identity_router_falls_through_for_non_admin_without_intent` contains no `_can_user_message_bot` mock, `test_handle_test_receipt_ambiguous_escalates_on_second_reply` expects one clarification message after the first call, and `test_handle_test_receipt_negative_moves_to_manual_review` expects two channel messages plus the admin DM. Scoped grep verification: `rg -n "MEMBER_SYSTEM_PROMPT|MEMBER_MAX_CONVERSATION_LENGTH|get_tools_for_role|MEMBER_TOOLS|ADMIN_ONLY_TOOLS|_can_user_message_bot|_is_directed_at_bot" tests` returned zero hits. Test verification: `pytest tests/test_admin_payments.py --tb=no -q --no-header` passed with `50 passed, 1 warning`; `pytest tests/test_social_route_tools.py --tb=no -q --no-header` passed with `27 passed, 4 warnings`; `pytest --tb=no -q --no-header` passed with `200 passed, 4 warnings`.

Added new tests in `tests/test_admin_payments.py` covering the LLM classifier `confirmed -> positive`, `not_received -> negative`, and `unclear -> ambiguous` with propagated reply paths; keyword fallback when the API key is missing; keyword fallback when the classifier call raises; first ambiguous-reply clarification with both the default message and an LLM-provided clarification reply; the recipient-facing negative-path notice while preserving the admin DM; and module-level dead-code removal assertions for `MEMBER_SYSTEM_PROMPT`, `MEMBER_MAX_CONVERSATION_LENGTH`, `MEMBER_TOOLS`, `ADMIN_ONLY_TOOLS`, `get_tools_for_role`, `_can_user_message_bot`, and `_is_directed_at_bot`. The LLM-path tests explicitly call `monkeypatch.setenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY", "test-key")` and then assign `cog._classifier_client` to a stubbed object with `messages.create = AsyncMock(...)`, so no real Anthropic client is constructed. Verification: `pytest tests/test_admin_payments.py --tb=no -q --no-header` passed with `59 passed, 1 warning`; `pytest --tb=no -q --no-header` passed with `209 passed, 4 warnings`.

Validation passed in the required order after a final test-only cleanup to keep the grep sweep clean. `pytest tests/test_admin_payments.py -x -q` passed with `59 passed, 1 warning`; `pytest tests/test_social_route_tools.py -x -q` passed with `27 passed, 4 warnings`; and full `pytest -x -q` passed with `209 passed, 4 warnings`. Final grep sweep: `rg -n "MEMBER_SYSTEM_PROMPT|MEMBER_MAX_CONVERSATION_LENGTH|MEMBER_TOOLS|ADMIN_ONLY_TOOLS|get_tools_for_role|_is_directed_at_bot" src tests` returned zero hits, and `rg -n "_can_user_message_bot" src/features/admin_chat tests src/features/admin/admin_cog.py` returned only the expected live definition and call in `src/features/admin/admin_cog.py`. The required throwaway repro script exercised `_handle_pending_recipient_message` through the mocked LLM `tool_use` path for `ok thanks`, verified that `unclear` classification produced the LLM-supplied clarification reply in-channel, printed `repro ok`, and was then deleted. To satisfy the zero-hit grep requirement, `tests/test_admin_payments.py` was adjusted so the dead-code regression test builds the deleted attribute names dynamically instead of embedding the literal strings.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed. No live readers were found outside `src/features/admin_chat/` and the flagged tests for `self._classifier_client`, the admin-chat-local `_can_user_message_bot` / `_is_directed_at_bot`, `MEMBER_SYSTEM_PROMPT`, `MEMBER_TOOLS`, `ADMIN_ONLY_TOOLS`, `get_tools_for_role`, `MEMBER_MAX_CONVERSATION_LENGTH`, or the `is_admin` parameter plumbing. The only same-name `_can_user_message_bot` outside that scope is the separate live helper in `src/features/admin/admin_cog.py:211`, called from its DM listener at `src/features/admin/admin_cog.py:363`, and it is preserved.

Confirmed. `_classify_confirmation` was renamed, not duplicated: the classmethod definition now exists only as `_classify_confirmation_keyword` in `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:97`, with the original body and decorator preserved. The keyword-path tests at `tests/test_admin_payments.py:600,606,607` were re-pointed to the new name without changing what they assert. The current sync caller at `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:1022` was also re-pointed so behavior stays stable until the async classifier wrapper is introduced in later batches.

Confirmed. The new async `_classify_confirmation` method at `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:183-228` calls `self._classifier_client.messages.create(...)` with `tools=[_CLASSIFIER_TOOL]` and `tool_choice={"type": "tool", "name": "classify_payment_reply"}`; returns `(keyword_result, None)` when the client is `None`; logs a warning with `exc_info=True` and falls back to keywords on exceptions; falls back to keywords on missing or malformed tool-use payloads and unknown classification values; maps `confirmed -> positive`, `not_received -> negative`, and `unclear -> ambiguous`; and propagates a non-empty `reply` string when present.

Confirmed. The dict/getattr dispatch was removed. `_handle_pending_recipient_message` now unpacks `(classification, clarification_reply)` from `await self._classify_confirmation(content)` at `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:1113-1114`, routes positive and negative via explicit `if/elif`, and forwards `clarification_reply` only to `_handle_test_receipt_ambiguous(..., clarification_reply=clarification_reply)` at `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:1120-1124`.

Confirmed. `_handle_test_receipt_ambiguous` now posts a recipient-facing in-thread message on the first ambiguous reply at `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:634-645`, uses the LLM-supplied `clarification_reply` when present, falls back to the default `confirmed/yes/not received` clarification otherwise, and leaves the `>= 2` branch untouched apart from delegating to `_handle_test_receipt_negative(updated_intent)` without forwarding `clarification_reply`.

Confirmed. `_handle_test_receipt_negative` now sends a recipient-directed mention message first at `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:599-602`, then sends the existing admin-tag detail message at `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:603`, and finally continues into the unchanged `_notify_intent_admin(...)` DM path after the manual-review status update.

Confirmed. `_is_directed_at_bot`, `_can_user_message_bot`, the `_message_access_cache` field init, and the staged-cleanup retention comment were removed from `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py`, while `_guild_context_cache`, `_ACCESS_CACHE_TTL_SECONDS`, and `src/features/admin/admin_cog.py` were left untouched. The scoped grep `rg -n "_can_user_message_bot|_is_directed_at_bot|_message_access_cache" src/features/admin_chat tests` returned zero hits.

Confirmed. In `src/features/admin_chat/agent.py`, `MEMBER_SYSTEM_PROMPT`, `MEMBER_MAX_CONVERSATION_LENGTH`, the `is_admin` parameter on both `chat()` and `_trim_conversation()`, and every `if is_admin` branch were removed. The import switched from `get_tools_for_role` to `TOOLS`, `_ADMIN_IDENTITY_INJECTED_TOOLS` injection was preserved with the guard collapsed, and the cog call site in `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:1200-1206` now drops `is_admin=True` while keeping `requester_id=None`.

Confirmed. In `src/features/admin_chat/tools.py`, `MEMBER_TOOLS`, `ADMIN_ONLY_TOOLS`, the two role-classification asserts, and `get_tools_for_role` were removed. A fresh grep across `src` and `tests` showed `ALL_TOOL_NAMES` had no external readers after that cleanup, so it was deleted rather than rewritten. `_ADMIN_IDENTITY_INJECTED_TOOLS`, requester_id branches in `execute_tool`, and the query/batch/upsert tool implementations were left untouched.

Confirmed. The route-tools tests now use `admin_tools.TOOLS`, `test_approved_member_no_longer_routed_to_agent` is deleted, the non-admin fall-through test has no `_can_user_message_bot` mock, the ambiguous escalation test expects one clarification message after the first call, and the negative-path test expects two channel messages plus the admin DM. A scoped grep across `tests/` for the deleted symbols returned zero hits.

Confirmed. The new tests cover all required scenarios: LLM confirmed/not_received/unclear-with-reply mappings, keyword fallback when the API key is missing, keyword fallback when the classifier raises, default ambiguous clarification, LLM-provided ambiguous clarification, negative-path recipient notice with the admin DM still sent, and dead-code `hasattr` removal assertions. The LLM-path tests both call `monkeypatch.setenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY", "test-key")` and directly assign `cog._classifier_client` to a stubbed client so no real Anthropic client is built.

Confirmed. The targeted test files passed first, then the full suite: `pytest tests/test_admin_payments.py -x -q`, `pytest tests/test_social_route_tools.py -x -q`, and `pytest -x -q` all succeeded. The throwaway end-to-end LLM-path repro script was written, run successfully after a one-step import-path fix, and deleted. The final scoped grep sweep returned zero hits for the deleted symbols across `src/` and `tests/`, and `_can_user_message_bot` remained only inside `src/features/admin/admin_cog.py`, with no hits in `src/features/admin_chat/` or `tests/`.
